meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real-world value.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a functional SaaS pricing engine. By integrating Stripe API with a robust backend, you demonstrate architectural maturity. This project proves you can handle complex financial logic, security, and user management—exactly what hiring managers look for in high-level engineering talent.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the ultimate career bottleneck. You have the technical skills, but without a commercial-grade project, you are just another applicant in a sea of theoretical knowledge. The industry doesn't care about another to-do list app; they care about revenue, subscription logic, and real-world billing architecture.

Today, we stop building toys and start building infrastructure. By architecting a professional SaaS pricing model, you shift the narrative from 'student' to 'systems engineer.' We will use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to refine our logic, turning a complex billing requirement into the centerpiece of your professional portfolio.

steps.title: Define the Subscription Architecture
steps.description: Establish the database schema for tiered pricing (Free, Pro, Enterprise). Focus on idempotency and state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS database schema using Prisma for a subscription model with three tiers, including fields for Stripe subscription IDs, status, and current period end.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tier SaaS subscription service with user-to-subscription mapping.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Securely handle events from Stripe to sync local database states with subscription changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events: customer.subscription.created, updated, and deleted. Include signature verification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a robust Stripe webhook listener in TypeScript with proper signature verification and error logging.'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Component
steps.description: Create a responsive, conversion-optimized pricing table that dynamically reflects API-driven product data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React pricing component with Tailwind CSS that fetches product tiers from the backend and handles loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a reusable React pricing table component with Tailwind CSS that displays subscription tiers fetched from the API.'

steps.title: Integrate Checkout Session Logic
steps.description: Connect the frontend to the Stripe SDK to initiate server-side checkout sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a backend controller to create a Stripe Checkout session for a specific price ID, ensuring user authentication is verified.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Implement a backend endpoint that creates a Stripe Checkout session, associating the session with the authenticated user ID.'

steps.title: Add Middleware Protection
steps.description: Implement route-level protection based on subscription status to enforce feature gating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks the user's subscription status in the database and redirects to the pricing page if access is denied.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a middleware that validates subscription status against a PostgreSQL database for protected API routes.'

pro_tips: Always store currency in the smallest unit (cents) to prevent floating-point arithmetic errors in financial calculations.
Implement 'soft-deletes' for subscriptions to allow for easy restoration and better audit trails.
Use Stripe's Test Mode to simulate edge cases like failed payments and expired cards; this shows senior-level foresight.
Add a 'usage-based' metric to your pricing model to demonstrate familiarity with modern consumption-based SaaS trends.
Document your API endpoints using OpenAPI/Swagger; it adds instant professional credibility to any GitHub repo.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy it to Vercel or Railway and add a 'Case Study' README section explaining the architecture decisions you made during this build.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect project to fall into your lap. You now have the skills to build the infrastructure that companies pay six-figure salaries to maintain. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
